data.description: The job search that runs on your machine. AI job application framework built on Claude Code: evaluate postings, tailor CVs, write cover letters, prep interviews. Fork it and own it. - MadsLorentzen/ai-job-search

The core workflow (self-profiling, fit evaluation, and the drafter-reviewer application pipeline) is \u003cstrong\u003elanguage- and country-agnostic\u003c/strong\u003e. The job portal search skills are built for the Danish market (Jobindex, Jobnet, Akademikernes Jobbank, etc.), but the pattern is designed to be swapped for your local job boards.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"snippet-clipboard-content notranslate position-relative overflow-auto\" data-snippet-clipboard-copy-content=\"/setup          /scrape              /apply The CV compiles with \u003ccode\u003elualatex\u003c/code\u003e (pdflatex often fails on modern MiKTeX installs with \u003ccode\u003efontawesome5\u003c/code\u003e font-expansion errors); the cover letter compiles with \u003ccode\u003exelatex\u003c/code\u003e because \u003ccode\u003ecover.cls\u003c/code\u003e requires \u003ccode\u003efontspec\u003c/code\u003e. If using a minimal TeX install such as TinyTeX or BasicTeX, install the extra packages listed in \u003ca href=\"/MadsLorentzen/ai-job-search/blob/master/SETUP.md#minimal-tex-install-tinytexbasictex\"\u003eSETUP.md\u003c/a\u003e.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eOptional: \u003ccode\u003epdftotext\u003c/code\u003e from \u003ca href=\"https://poppler.freedesktop.org/\" rel=\"nofollow\"\u003epoppler\u003c/a\u003e (macOS: \u003ccode\u003ebrew install poppler\u003c/code\u003e, Debian/Ubuntu: \u003ccode\u003eapt install poppler-utils\u003c/code\u003e, Windows: \u003ccode\u003echoco install poppler\u003c/code\u003e) — used by \u003ccode\u003e/apply\u003c/code\u003e's ATS parseability check on the compiled CV. \u003ccode\u003e/apply\u003c/code\u003e form the core workflow. Seven more commands extend it once your profile is in place:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003e\u003ccode\u003e/interview\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/strong\u003e preps you for a scheduled interview on a tracked application. It builds a stage-specific prep pack from the application's archive (the exact posting, the CV and cover letter the interviewer actually read, feedback recorded from earlier rounds), researches the company and interviewers with a verify-before-use rule, maps likely questions to your STAR examples, and offers a mock interview following the roleplay protocol in \u003ccode\u003e07-interview-prep.md\u003c/code\u003e. Gaps get honest bridge answers, never invented experience.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003e\u003ccode\u003e/outcome\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/strong\u003e records what happened to an application - interview stages, offers, rejections, silence. It archives the submitted CV, cover letter, and posting text into \u003ccode\u003edocuments/applications/\u0026lt;company\u0026gt;_\u0026lt;role\u0026gt;/\u003c/code\u003e, keeps \u003ccode\u003eoutcome.md\u003c/code\u003e in the format \u003ccode\u003e/setup\u003c/code\u003e Path A parses, and updates the tracker. Once a few applications resolve, it points you back to \u003ccode\u003e/setup\u003c/code\u003e to calibrate the fit framework from what actually got interviews.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003e\u003ccode\u003e/rank\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/strong\u003e bridges \u003ccode\u003e/scrape\u003c/code\u003e and \u003ccode\u003e/apply\u003c/code\u003e: it batch-scores all newly scraped postings against the fit framework (parallel agents fetch each posting and score the five evaluation dimensions) and returns a ranked shortlist with honest per-job strengths and gaps. Deal-breakers veto, deadlines get urgency flags, dead postings get marked expired. Pick a number and it hands off to the full \u003ccode\u003e/apply\u003c/code\u003e workflow.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003e\u003ccode\u003e/expand\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/strong\u003e enriches your profile by scanning public sources you've already linked in it (GitHub repos, portfolio site, Kaggle, Google Scholar) and looking up syllabi for named courses and certifications. Discovered competencies are added to your profile with a source tag. Useful right after \u003ccode\u003e/setup\u003c/code\u003e to surface skills that documents alone don't make explicit.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003e\u003ccode\u003e/upskill\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/strong\u003e analyzes the gap between your profile and your tracked job postings (or a single posting via \u003ccode\u003e/upskill \u0026lt;URL\u0026gt;\u003c/code\u003e). Produces a prioritized heatmap of skill gaps and a learning plan with web-searched study resources and time estimates. Useful for career planning between applications.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003e\u003ccode\u003e/add-template\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/strong\u003e registers your own LaTeX CV or cover letter template in place of the stock ones. It captures the template's instructions (compile engine, fonts, style rules, page limit), runs a mandatory test compile, and wires the template into \u003ccode\u003e/apply\u003c/code\u003e. See \u003ca href=\"#latex-templates\"\u003eLaTeX templates\u003c/a\u003e below.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003e\u003ccode\u003e/add-portal\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/strong\u003e generates a job-portal search skill for a job board in your market. It investigates the portal (search URL pattern, result structure, access rules), scaffolds the CLI skill from the same structure as the shipped ones, and test-runs a live query before registering. See \u003ca href=\"#job-search-tools\"\u003eJob search tools\u003c/a\u003e below.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003e/reset\u003c/code\u003e is also available, see \u003ca href=\"#starting-over\"\u003eStarting over\u003c/a\u003e below.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch2 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch2 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003eHow \u003ccode\u003e/apply\u003c/code\u003e works\u003c/h2\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-how-apply-works\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: How /apply works\" href=\"#how-apply-works\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eThe \u003ccode\u003e/apply\u003c/code\u003e command runs a \u003cstrong\u003edrafter-reviewer workflow\u003c/strong\u003e with mandatory PDF compilation:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003col dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eParse\u003c/strong\u003e the job posting (URL or text)\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eEvaluate fit\u003c/strong\u003e against your profile (skills, experience, culture, location, career alignment)\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eDraft\u003c/strong\u003e a tailored CV and cover letter in LaTeX\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eSpawn a reviewer agent\u003c/strong\u003e that researches the company and critiques the drafts\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eRevise\u003c/strong\u003e based on the reviewer's feedback\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eCompile and inspect\u003c/strong\u003e both PDFs: lualatex for the CV, xelatex for the cover letter. Claude reads the rendered pages and iterates on the LaTeX until the CV is exactly 2 pages with no orphaned entry titles, and the cover letter is exactly 1 page with the signature visible and fonts consistent.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eATS-check the CV\u003c/strong\u003e: extract the PDF's text layer (\u003ccode\u003epdftotext\u003c/code\u003e, optional dependency) and verify it the way an ATS parser sees it — contact details present as literal text, no garbled glyphs, sane reading order — then score the posting's keyword coverage against the extraction. Keywords the profile genuinely supports get added; genuine gaps stay visible, never stuffed.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003ePresent\u003c/strong\u003e the final output with a verification checklist\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ol\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eAll claims in the CV and cover letter are verified against your actual profile. The system never fabricates skills or experience.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch3 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003eWhat makes this workflow different\u003c/h3\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-what-makes-this-workflow-different\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: What makes this workflow different\" href=\"#what-makes-this-workflow-different\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003ePDF verification loop.\u003c/strong\u003e Most LaTeX-resume templates produce \"looks fine in the .tex\" output that breaks in the PDF: job titles orphan to the next page, cover letters spill onto page 2, bullet fonts silently fall back to the body font. The \u003ccode\u003e/apply\u003c/code\u003e command compiles and visually inspects every PDF and applies targeted fixes (\u003ccode\u003e\\needspace\u003c/code\u003e, \u003ccode\u003e\\enlargethispage\u003c/code\u003e, font-matching wrappers for list items) until the layout is clean. This runs automatically on every application.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eATS verification on the PDF text layer.\u003c/strong\u003e An ATS reads the PDF's embedded text, not the rendered page — and LaTeX can silently produce PDFs whose text extracts as garbage (icon glyphs where the email should be, interleaved lines from multi-column layouts). \u003ccode\u003e/apply\u003c/code\u003e extracts the compiled CV's text layer with \u003ccode\u003epdftotext\u003c/code\u003e and verifies contact details, reading order, and the posting's keyword coverage against what a parser actually sees. Honesty rule enforced: a keyword the profile doesn't support is acknowledged as a gap, never stuffed in.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eRelevance-weighted CV cutting.\u003c/strong\u003e When a CV overflows 2 pages, the workflow does not cut mechanically from the \"oldest\" section. It scores each candidate line by (a) relevance to the target posting, (b) uniqueness in the document, and (c) whether the cover letter depends on it, and cuts the lowest-total-score line first. An older-role bullet that hits posting keywords survives ahead of a recent-role bullet that does not.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eDrafter-reviewer separation.\u003c/strong\u003e The drafter writes; a second Claude agent, spawned with a fresh context, researches the company and critiques the drafts. The drafter then revises. This catches missed keywords, weak framing, and generic language that a single pass often leaves in.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eToken-efficient reviewer dispatch.\u003c/strong\u003e The reviewer agent receives drafts inline rather than re-reading them, and the verification checklist runs once at the end of the workflow rather than being duplicated by both agents. Note: the new compile-and-inspect step in Step 5 spends some of those savings on PDF rendering and layout iteration — the workflow trades some end-to-end token cost for a real reduction in broken PDFs reaching the user.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch2 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" class=\"notranslate\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003e/setup --section search\n\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/pre\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eThis re-runs the search configuration interview: which roles to target, which skills to search for, which locations, and which portals. It also suggests role types you may not have considered based on your profile.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch3 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003eLaTeX templates\u003c/h3\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-latex-templates\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: LaTeX templates\" href=\"#latex-templates\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eThe CV uses \u003ca href=\"https://ctan.org/pkg/moderncv\" rel=\"nofollow\"\u003emoderncv\u003c/a\u003e (banking style). The cover letter uses a custom \u003ccode\u003ecover.cls\u003c/code\u003e with Lato/Raleway fonts.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eTo use your own template instead, run:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"snippet-clipboard-content notranslate position-relative overflow-auto\" data-snippet-clipboard-copy-content=\"/add-template\"\u003e\u003cpre class=\"notranslate\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003e/add-template\n\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/pre\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003ePoint it at your \u003ccode\u003e.tex\u003c/code\u003e file (plus any \u003ccode\u003e.cls\u003c/code\u003e/\u003ccode\u003e.sty\u003c/code\u003e files or bundled fonts). The command interviews you for the template's instructions — compile engine, fonts and where they live, style rules to preserve, hard page limit — stores everything under \u003ccode\u003etemplates/\u003c/code\u003e, runs a mandatory test compile, and activates the template so \u003ccode\u003e/apply\u003c/code\u003e drafts from it. Templates are stored with \u003ccode\u003e[PLACEHOLDER]\u003c/code\u003e tokens instead of personal data, so they're safe to commit and share.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003ccode\u003e/add-template --list\u003c/code\u003e shows registered templates\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003ccode\u003e/add-template --use \u0026lt;name\u0026gt;\u003c/code\u003e switches between them\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003ccode\u003e/add-template --use default\u003c/code\u003e reverts to the stock moderncv / cover.cls templates\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eIf you prefer doing it by hand, the manual route still works: update the guidance in \u003ccode\u003e05-cv-templates.md\u003c/code\u003e and \u003ccode\u003e06-cover-letter-templates.md\u003c/code\u003e.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch3 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003eJob search tools\u003c/h3\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-job-search-tools\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: Job search tools\" href=\"#job-search-tools\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eThe four Danish CLI tools in \u003ccode\u003e.agents/skills/\u003c/code\u003e (Jobbank, Jobdanmark, Jobindex, Jobnet) demonstrate the pattern for building a job-portal integration for a specific market. If you're in a different country, run:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"snippet-clipboard-content notranslate position-relative overflow-auto\" data-snippet-clipboard-copy-content=\"/add-portal\"\u003e\u003cpre class=\"notranslate\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003e/add-portal\n\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/pre\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eGive it your local job board's URL. The command investigates the portal (search-URL pattern, result-page structure, robots.txt/access rules), scaffolds a CLI skill with the same structure, commands, and output contract as the shipped ones, and test-runs a live query before registering anything. Auth-walled portals are declined, and portals with restrictive terms get a prominent personal-use-only warning in the generated skill. The generated skill is market-specific and lives in your fork; the generator itself is the universal part.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eMaintaining a fork adapted to your market or language? Add it to the \u003ca href=\"https://github.com/MadsLorentzen/ai-job-search/discussions/78\" data-hovercard-type=\"discussion\" data-hovercard-url=\"/MadsLorentzen/ai-job-search/discussions/78/hovercard\"\u003eCommunity forks \u0026amp; adaptations\u003c/a\u003e thread so others can find it.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eFor \u003cstrong\u003ecountry-agnostic\u003c/strong\u003e starting points outside Denmark, the repo ships two portal skills alongside the Danish demos:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003e\u003ccode\u003elinkedin-search\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/strong\u003e — built on LinkedIn's public, unauthenticated \u003ccode\u003ejobs-guest\u003c/code\u003e endpoints. Field-agnostic, \u003cstrong\u003ezero runtime dependencies\u003c/strong\u003e (runs with just \u003ccode\u003ebun\u003c/code\u003e), and takes the search location as an explicit flag, so it works for any market out of the box (\u003ccode\u003e-l \"Berlin, Germany\"\u003c/code\u003e, \u003ccode\u003e-l \"Mumbai, Maharashtra, India\"\u003c/code\u003e, \u003ccode\u003e-l \"Remote\"\u003c/code\u003e, …). Intended for \u003cstrong\u003epersonal use only\u003c/strong\u003e — automated access is against LinkedIn's Terms of Service, so keep volume low. See \u003ccode\u003e.agents/skills/linkedin-search/SKILL.md\u003c/code\u003e.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003e\u003ccode\u003efreehire-search\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/strong\u003e — queries the \u003ca href=\"https://freehire.dev\" rel=\"nofollow\"\u003efreehire.dev\u003c/a\u003e aggregator's public REST API (JSON, no API key). Tech-focused (software, data, engineering, DevOps, remote), multi-market via facet flags (\u003ccode\u003e--region\u003c/code\u003e, \u003ccode\u003e--country\u003c/code\u003e, \u003ccode\u003e--remote\u003c/code\u003e), and \u003cstrong\u003ezero runtime dependencies\u003c/strong\u003e. Unlike the HTML-scraping Danish portals, results come back structured (skills, seniority, category). The backend is MIT-licensed and \u003ca href=\"https://github.com/strelov1/freehire\"\u003eself-hostable\u003c/a\u003e — point \u003ccode\u003eFREEHIRE_API_URL\u003c/code\u003e at your own instance if you prefer. See \u003ccode\u003e.agents/skills/freehire-search/SKILL.md\u003c/code\u003e.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch3 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003eSalary benchmarking\u003c/h3\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-salary-benchmarking\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: Salary benchmarking\" href=\"#salary-benchmarking\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eThe salary tool works with any salary data you provide (union statistics, Glassdoor exports, personal research, etc.). See \u003ccode\u003etools/README_SALARY_TOOL.md\u003c/code\u003e for the expected format and setup. If you don't have salary data, the salary step is simply skipped.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch3 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003eStarting over\u003c/h3\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-starting-over\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: Starting over\" href=\"#starting-over\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eTo wipe your profile data and start fresh:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"snippet-clipboard-content notranslate position-relative overflow-auto\" data-snippet-clipboard-copy-content=\"/reset profile    # clears skill files, preserves framework rules\n/reset documents  # deletes files from documents/ folder\n/reset all        # both\"\u003e\u003cpre class=\"notranslate\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003e/reset profile    # clears skill files, preserves framework rules\n/reset documents  # deletes files from documents/ folder\n/reset all        # both\n\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/pre\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003e/reset\u003c/code\u003e shows exactly what will be deleted and requires you to type \u003ccode\u003eRESET\u003c/code\u003e to confirm. Nothing is deleted until you do.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch2 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003eTips for better dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch3 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003eProfile depth matters\u003c/h3\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-profile-depth-matters\" class=\"anchor\" 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eThe single biggest factor in output quality is how much detail you put into your profile. A thin profile produces generic applications; a detailed one enables genuinely tailored results.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eRole descriptions:\u003c/strong\u003e Don't just list job titles. Describe what you actually did in each position: specific projects, tools used, responsibilities, and measurable achievements. The more material you provide, the more precisely the system can reframe your experience for different roles.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eSkills in context:\u003c/strong\u003e Instead of listing \"Python\" or \"project management,\" describe how and where you applied them. \"Built ML pipelines for customer churn prediction in Python using scikit-learn\" gives the system far more to work with than \"Python, machine learning.\"\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eAll onboarding paths work:\u003c/strong\u003e Whether you point \u003ccode\u003e/setup\u003c/code\u003e at your \u003ccode\u003edocuments/\u003c/code\u003e folder, paste a single CV, or walk through the interview, the principle is the same: richer input produces sharper output.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch3 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003eCareer path discovery\u003c/h3\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-career-path-discovery\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: Career path discovery\" href=\"#career-path-discovery\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eThe framework supports two distinct modes of job searching:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eExplicit targeting:\u003c/strong\u003e You know which roles or sectors you want. The system helps refine and prioritize based on fit.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eLatent opportunity discovery:\u003c/strong\u003e By analyzing your full history (not just job titles, but the actual work you did), the system can surface career paths you haven't considered. Transferable skills that map to unexpected industries, patterns in what you enjoyed or excelled at, or emerging roles that combine your domain expertise with new technology.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eTo get the most from this, invest time during \u003ccode\u003e/setup\u003c/code\u003e in describing not just your experience, but what energized you, what drained you, and what you'd want more of. This context directly shapes how the system evaluates fit and which roles it surfaces during \u003ccode\u003e/scrape\u003c/code\u003e.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch2 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003eContributing\u003c/h2\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-contributing\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: Contributing\" href=\"#contributing\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eThinking about a PR? Rebased onto current master. The SKILL.md description and "How It Works"
copy were already generalized by #102, so this drops those now-redundant
edits and keeps only the unique remaining value: replacing the hardcoded
Danish job boards (jobindex.dk, karriere.dk, jobfinder.dk, akademikernes.dk)
and Danish location terms in search-queries.md with configurable
placeholders and an /add-portal pointer, so the query templates apply to
any market.

test suite — CONTRIBUTING.md asks for tests CI never executes (#100)

* ci: run the Python test suite - CONTRIBUTING.md asks for tests CI never executes

CONTRIBUTING.md tells contributors to put Python tool tests in tests/
and run the relevant suites, and tests/ now holds real ones
(test_salary_lookup.py, test_convert_salary_excel.py from #75) - but no
CI job executes them. A suite that never runs in CI can't gate a PR and
silently rots. New python-tests job: unittest discover over tests/,
stdlib only, no new dependencies. Future test files run without any
workflow change.

Also lands tests/test_security_guards.py, which missed #84's merge
window (pushed to the branch as #84 was being merged; the merge took
2a6cb8c, the tests were 260c37a). 13 unittest cases in the existing
tests/ style: each copies the guard script into a synthetic repo tree
and runs it as a subprocess - the same way CI invokes it - asserting
real exit codes and messages. Every forbidden state fails (Bash(*) and
Bash(curl:*) additions, each personal-data gitignore rule removed one
at a time, each forbidden lifecycle script, trustedDependencies,
invalid settings JSON, zero manifests); every non-event passes (dropped
shipped permission, extra ignore rules, benign scripts, hostile
manifest inside node_modules); and the real repo passes its own guards.

22 tests total, all passing locally via the exact command the job runs.

* test: use benign lifecycle-script values in fixtures - AV heuristics flag attack-shaped strings

Review found the curl-pipe-to-sh fixture value matches a real Defender
signature (Trojan:Script/Stealer.HAX!MTB): Windows quarantines the temp
package.json mid-test, making the suite flaky for any Windows
contributor who runs it - while proving nothing extra, since the guard
flags the script KEY and never inspects the value.

Fixture values are now 'echo test' (also in the node_modules-ignored
test, same class of string), with a comment on the key-only test
explaining why the value must stay benign so a future 'make the fixture
realistic' cleanup doesn't reintroduce the quarantine flake. Coverage
is unchanged: same keys, same assertions, 13 tests passing." class="Link--secondary" href="/MadsLorentzen/ai-job-search/commit/cd7c22325b73a21cb51dc9ccef79691b73d5265a">ci: run the Python test suite — CONTRIBUTING.md asks for tests CI nev…</a></div></div></td><td><div class="react-directory-commit-age"><relative-time data-component="RelativeTime" tense="past" datetime="2026-07-10T06:06:00.000Z" GitHub Actions workflow - LaTeX smoke compiles, skill lint, CLI typechecks, placeholder integrity (#59)

Every PR to this repo is currently verified by hand. This adds the checks
a machine can do:

- latex-smoke: compiles cv/main_example.tex (lualatex) and the new
  cover_letters/cover_example.tex (xelatex) in the texlive/texlive
  container, failing on any LaTeX error. Exact page-count assertions
  (CV=2, cover letter=1) run on the upstream repo only
- lint (tools/lint_skills.py, also runnable locally): every SKILL.md has
  parseable YAML frontmatter with name+description (frontmatter breakage
  happened before - 37a0eed), allowed-tools 'bun run <path>' targets
  exist, command files start with a '# /<name>' title, settings.json is
  valid JSON with a permissions.allow list
- cli-typecheck: bun install + tsc --noEmit for all five portal CLIs
  (matrix, fail-fast off)
- placeholder-integrity (upstream only): tracked template files still
  carry their placeholder tokens, catching accidental personal-data
  commits before they land

Fork-friendly by design: /setup personalizes CLAUDE.md, the skill files,
and main_example.tex in forks, so placeholder checks and exact page
counts are guarded with github.repository == upstream; compile success
and lint run everywhere. Live CLI smoke tests are deliberately excluded:
network-flaky, and linkedin-search is personal-use-only per its own ToS
warning - CI-automated requests would violate it. CLIs are typechecked
instead.

The cover letter previously had no tracked example (cover_*.tex is
gitignored), so cover_example.tex is new: a placeholder letter following
the documented 06 structure, demonstrating the correct itemize-outside-
lettercontent pattern. It doubles as the structural reference /apply
Step 2 looks for on fresh clones, which until now matched nothing. The
gitignore exception is ordered after Cover_*.tex because case-insensitive
filesystems match that pattern against cover_example.tex too.

Writing it surfaced a latent bug in the documented template itself:
06-cover-letter-templates.md's structure ends with \closing{Kind
regards,\} - but cover.cls appends its own \, and the doubled break
produces '! LaTeX Error: There's no line here to end.' on every compile
(nonstopmode swallows it, so it went unnoticed). Fixed in 06 and noted
in the example." class="Link--secondary" href="/MadsLorentzen/ai-job-search/commit/6477987608da99ae3762bcd9fd9bf2e0509dfc29">ci: add GitHub Actions workflow - LaTeX smoke compiles, skill lint, class="react-directory-commit-message"><a data-pjax="true" title="fix(cv): override moderncv banking colors and remove inter-item \vspace

Two fixes for the moderncv banking + lualatex + MiKTeX combination that
the README documents as the recommended setup:

1. **Color overrides for names and section headings.** Default banking on
   modern MiKTeX renders firstname, lastname, and \section headings in
   black, which clashes with the rest of the blue accent scheme (links,
   bullets, contact icons). Add three \renewcommand* lines that force
   firstnamestyle, lastnamestyle, and sectionstyle to use \color{color1}.
   Both names render bold in matching blue, and every \section heading
   renders blue with the standard horizontal underline. No effect for
   users on a setup where the defaults already render blue.

2. **Remove \vspace{1pt} between \item entries in itemize lists.** This
   pattern (used 21 times in the template) intermittently produces an
   oversized gap before a single item: every adjacent pair has the same
   separator in the source, but LaTeX renders one of them noticeably
   wider because the inter-item \vspace creates a paragraph break that
   interacts with the list's internal \itemsep. Removing the inter-item
   \vspace lets itemize use its native uniform spacing, which renders
   consistently. \vspace{1pt} immediately after \section{...} (between
   header and first item) and \vspace{3pt} between top-level \cventry
   blocks are kept - those are different patterns and render fine.

Also updated the file's compile-comment from "pdflatex" to "lualatex"
to match the README.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>" class="Link--secondary" href="/MadsLorentzen/ai-job-search/commit/3f8d04d759930b75cbaa53ee6df6491394f59e8d">fix(cv): override /interview command for stage-specific interview prep from the application archive (#58)

07-interview-prep.md holds STAR examples, tough-question patterns,
questions to ask, and a roleplay protocol - and the job-application-
assistant skill names interview preparation as its Step 4 - but no
command operationalizes any of it. /apply ends at 'files ready', and the
moment an interview is scheduled, nothing picks the thread back up.
/interview does, drawing on the archive /outcome now maintains.

/apply optimizes what the company reads; /interview optimizes what the
company hears. The bridge is consistency: the interviewer read the
submitted CV and cover letter, so prep must match what they claim.

How it works:

- Loads the application context from documents/applications/
  <company>_<role>/: the exact posting, the submitted drafts, and
  outcome.md's stage + recorded feedback (stage-N feedback is the
  highest-priority input for stage-N+1 prep). Graceful fallbacks for
  applications that predate /outcome, stating plainly what is missing
- Runs the Company Research Checklist that 04-job-evaluation.md defines
  but nothing executes, plus interviewer-angle notes from public
  professional info only. Every company claim is independently verified
  before it enters the pack - same rule as cover-letter claims
- Builds a stage-specific prep pack: likely questions (earlier-stage
  feedback first, then fit-evaluation gaps with honest bridge answers
  per 07's you-don't-have-X pattern, then posting requirements, then
  stage type), STAR mapping via 07's Use-for tags with new drafts
  grounded strictly in profile facts, a consistency brief of the
  submitted documents' probeable claims, customized tough questions,
  4-6 questions to ask, logistics tips
- Saves the pack to documents/applications/<company>_<role>/
  interview_prep_<stage>.md - one per stage, kept as history,
  gitignored with the rest of the archive
- Offers a mock interview following 07's Roleplay Guidelines verbatim,
  with feedback calibrated against the behavioral profile (same
  voice-consistency rule the /apply reviewer applies)
- Never edits framework or profile files, except appending
  user-approved STAR examples to 07 on explicit request

Integration: /outcome suggests /interview when an interview stage is
recorded; documents/README.md documents the interview_prep_<stage>.md
files (and that /setup ignores them); README commands list and file
tree." class="Link--secondary" href="/MadsLorentzen/ai-job-search/commit/75fbfc56e465d59bf87f5cfec5defc8c83207575">feat: add /interview command for stage-specific interview prep from class="react-directory-row-commit-cell"><div><div class="react-directory-commit-message"><a data-pjax="true" title="feat: add /add-template command for registering custom LaTeX templates (#30)

Users could already swap the stock moderncv/cover.cls templates, but only by
hand-editing the guidance in 05-cv-templates.md and 06-cover-letter-templates.md.
/add-template automates that:

- Interviews the user for the template's instructions: compile engine, fonts
  (bundled files or system), style rules to preserve, and hard page limit
- Stores the template profile-agnostic ([PLACEHOLDER] tokens) under templates/
  with a TEMPLATE.md manifest, so templates are safe to commit and share
- Runs a mandatory test compile with dummy data before registering anything
- Activates via a single managed block in 05/06, which /apply already reads,
  so no changes to the /apply workflow are needed; --use default is a clean
  revert to the stock templates
- --list and --use <name> manage multiple registered templates

Docs: README (commands list, file structure, LaTeX templates section) and
SETUP.md (compile section pointer)." class="Link--secondary" href="/MadsLorentzen/ai-job-search/commit/6b144dc456cfb2b2617c82e80b757e92e37b6b15">feat: add /add-template command for registering custom class="react-directory-row-commit-cell"><div><div class="react-directory-commit-message"><a data-pjax="true" title="feat: add /setup_docs, /reset, /expand commands and /upskill skill

Adds four contributions from @Michael-Bach:

- /setup_docs - document-driven profile population from a documents/ folder (CV, LinkedIn export, diplomas, references, past applications). Idempotent merge with explicit additive vs. conflicting buckets and per-conflict prompts.
- /reset - typed-RESET confirmation gate for clearing profile data and/or documents folder contents.
- /expand - additive competency enrichment from documents and public URLs already in the profile (GitHub repos, portfolio sites), with web-searched syllabus lookups for named courses and certifications.
- /upskill - skill-gap analysis vs tracked jobs (or single URL), produces a prioritized heatmap and learning plan with year-tagged WebSearch queries.

Also adds the documents/ folder convention with README and gitignore entries for personal output files.

Closes #6" class="Link--secondary" href="/MadsLorentzen/ai-job-search/commit/2ba441f3fb3c3f365377bc1a283a77793b33c924">feat: add /setup_docs, /reset, /expand commands and /upskill class="react-directory-commit-message"><a data-pjax="true" title="ci: add GitHub Actions workflow - LaTeX smoke compiles, skill lint, CLI typechecks, placeholder integrity (#59)

Every PR to this repo is currently verified by hand. This adds the checks
a machine can do:

- latex-smoke: compiles cv/main_example.tex (lualatex) and the new
  cover_letters/cover_example.tex (xelatex) in the texlive/texlive
  container, failing on any LaTeX error. Exact page-count assertions
  (CV=2, cover letter=1) run on the upstream repo only
- lint (tools/lint_skills.py, also runnable locally): every SKILL.md has
  parseable YAML frontmatter with name+description (frontmatter breakage
  happened before - 37a0eed), allowed-tools 'bun run <path>' targets
  exist, command files start with a '# /<name>' title, settings.json is
  valid JSON with a permissions.allow list
- cli-typecheck: bun install + tsc --noEmit for all five portal CLIs
  (matrix, fail-fast off)
- placeholder-integrity (upstream only): tracked template files still
  carry their placeholder tokens, catching accidental personal-data
  commits before they land

Fork-friendly by design: /setup personalizes CLAUDE.md, the skill files,
and main_example.tex in forks, so placeholder checks and exact page
counts are guarded with github.repository == upstream; compile success
and lint run everywhere. Live CLI smoke tests are deliberately excluded:
network-flaky, and linkedin-search is personal-use-only per its own ToS
warning - CI-automated requests would violate it. CLIs are typechecked
instead.

The cover letter previously had no tracked example (cover_*.tex is
gitignored), so cover_example.tex is new: a placeholder letter following
the documented 06 structure, demonstrating the correct itemize-outside-
lettercontent pattern. It doubles as the structural reference /apply
Step 2 looks for on fresh clones, which until now matched nothing. The
gitignore exception is ordered after Cover_*.tex because case-insensitive
filesystems match that pattern against cover_example.tex too.

Writing it surfaced a latent bug in the documented template itself:
06-cover-letter-templates.md's structure ends with \closing{Kind
regards,\} - but cover.cls appends its own \, and the doubled break
produces '! LaTeX Error: There's no line here to end.' on every compile
(nonstopmode swallows it, so it went unnoticed). Fixed in 06 and noted
in the example." class="Link--secondary" href="/MadsLorentzen/ai-job-search/commit/6477987608da99ae3762bcd9fd9bf2e0509dfc29">ci: add GitHub Actions workflow - LaTeX smoke compiles, skill lint, class="react-directory-commit-message"><a data-pjax="true" title="feat: add ATS & keyword verification pass to /apply's compile-and-inspect loop (#40)

An ATS reads the compiled PDF's embedded text layer, not the rendered page,
and LaTeX can silently produce PDFs whose text extracts as garbage: icon
glyphs where contact details should be, (cid:*) markers from fonts without
Unicode mappings, interleaved lines from multi-column layouts. This matters
more now that /add-template lets users bring arbitrary templates. The
existing Step 5 loop verifies what a human sees; this adds verification of
what a parser sees.

New Step 5d in /apply (CV only - cover letters rarely go through keyword
screening; cleanup renumbered to 5e):

- Extract the CV PDF's text layer with pdftotext -layout. pdftotext
  (poppler) is an optional dependency: if missing, the mechanical check is
  skipped with a warning and keyword coverage falls back to the visual PDF
  read - the same graceful-skip pattern as salary_lookup.py
- Parseability checks verified against a real extraction of the stock
  template: email/phone must survive as literal text (fontawesome icons
  extract as harmless glyph-name noise like MOBILE-ALT/Envelope, but a
  contact detail carried only by an icon or hyperlink is invisible to ATS),
  no (cid:*) or replacement-character garbage, reading order matching
  visual order, dates present
- Keyword coverage reuses the required/preferred list from Step 1, matched
  in the posting's language, reported as covered / synonym-only /
  missing-have-it / missing-gap. Honesty rule enforced: keywords the
  profile genuinely supports get added to experience bullets; genuine gaps
  stay visible, never stuffed

Integration: CLAUDE.md verification checklist section, ATS Parseability
guidance in 05-cv-templates.md, narrow Bash(pdftotext:*) entry in the
pre-approved permissions (keeping with the tightened scope from #27),
cv/*.txt gitignored (extraction is personal data; also deleted by the
step itself), and optional-dependency docs in README and SETUP." class="Link--secondary" href="/MadsLorentzen/ai-job-search/commit/2551824ecb68cc88e9c5581ec2015965a6acd2e3">feat: add ATS & keyword verification pass to /apply's compile-and-ins…</a></div></div></td><td><div class="react-directory-commit-age"><relative-time data-component="RelativeTime" tense="past" CONTRIBUTING.md PR/issue references clickable (#91)

GitHub auto-links #N only in issue/PR comments and commit messages, not
in rendered markdown files - so all 26 precedent references in
CONTRIBUTING.md ((#30), (#55, #56), ...) render as plain text, and the
reader can't jump to the precedent being cited.

Converted every bare #N to a reference-style link ([#N] in prose, URL
definitions collected at the bottom of the file). Definitions use the
/issues/N form, which GitHub redirects to /pull/N when the number is a
PR, so each link resolves correctly whether it points at an issue or a
pull request. No prose changed; existing inline links (the Discussions
URLs) untouched." class="Link--secondary" href="/MadsLorentzen/ai-job-search/commit/779a33aa20ad775d73a594a0f14dc1fa06a69515">docs: make CONTRIBUTING.md PR/issue references clickable (</a><a class="issue-link js-issue-link" data-error-text="Failed to load title" data-id="4843726568" data-permission-text="Title is private" data-url="https://github.com/MadsLorentzen/ai-job-search/issues/91" data-hovercard-type="pull_request" data-hovercard-url="/MadsLorentzen/ai-job-search/pull/91/hovercard" href="https://github.com/MadsLorentzen/ai-job-search/pull/91" aria-keyshortcuts="Alt+ArrowUp">#91</a><a data-pjax="true" title="docs: make CONTRIBUTING.md PR/issue references clickable (#91)

title="refactor(salary): optimize search match scoring and normalize Excel category keys (#101)

This commit improves the performance and consistency of the salary tools:

- Redundant query normalization and word extraction are eliminated in salary_lookup.py by pre-calculating representations once before the search loop.
- A match_score_optimized helper is introduced to perform the comparison using the pre-calculated query data, preserving full backward compatibility for match_score.
- Normalization in tools/convert_salary_excel.py is unified: paired column headers now consistently substitute spaces and dashes with underscores (e.g. 'software_engineering') to match the single-column formatting.
- Unit test coverage is significantly expanded in tests/test_salary_lookup.py and tests/test_convert_salary_excel.py to cover normalization, anglicization, search filtering, and matching behaviors." class="Link--secondary" href="/MadsLorentzen/ai-job-search/commit/c134eef5530e5431707ab553582a452be2306041">refactor(salary): optimize search match scoring and normalize Excel <div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h1 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">AI Job Search</h1><a id="user-content-ai-job-search" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: AI Job Search" href="#ai-job-search"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p align="center" dir="auto">
  <a href="https://trendshift.io/repositories/43622?utm_source=trendshift-badge&utm_medium=badge&utm_campaign=badge-trendshift-43622" rel="nofollow"><img src="https://camo.githubusercontent.com/45b4f6eb7dce41cb667494ce70a7597f288df37dc33dde0331193f7c8e147cb9/68747470733a2f2f7472656e6473686966742e696f2f6170692f62616467652f7472656e6473686966742f7265706f7369746f726965732f34333632322f6461696c79" alt="MadsLorentzen%2Fai-job-search | Trendshift" width="250" height="55" data-canonical-src="https://trendshift.io/api/badge/trendshift/repositories/43622/daily" style="max-width: 100%; height: auto; max-height: 55px;; aspect-ratio: 250 / 55; background-color: var(--bgColor-muted); border-radius: 6px" class="js-gh-image-fallback"></a>

<p dir="auto"><a href="https://github.com/MadsLorentzen/ai-job-search/actions/workflows/ci.yml"><img src="https://github.com/MadsLorentzen/ai-job-search/actions/workflows/ci.yml/badge.svg" alt="CI" style="max-width: 100%;"></a></p>
<p dir="auto">An AI-powered job application framework built on <a href="https://claude.com/claude-code" rel="nofollow">Claude Code</a>. Fork it, fill in your profile, and let Claude evaluate job postings, tailor your CV, write cover letters, and prepare you for interviews.</p>
<blockquote>
<p dir="auto">Note: This is an independent open-source project and is not affiliated with, endorsed by, sponsored by, or maintained by Anthropic. Anthropic and Claude Code are referenced only to describe the toolchain this workflow uses.</p>
</blockquote>
<p align="center" dir="auto">
  <i>Did this save you a Sunday of cover-letter writing? Consider a coffee.<br>
  Did it land you the job? Maybe two.</i> ☕
</p>
<p align="center" dir="auto">
  <a href="https://ko-fi.com/madslorentzen" rel="nofollow">
    <img src="https://camo.githubusercontent.com/b83b435a0fbb41274d1021b0d2747ae96c34c69f8cb51cc339b3eb5bbcca75a9/68747470733a2f2f73746f726167652e6b6f2d66692e636f6d2f63646e2f6b6f6669332e706e673f763d36" alt="Buy me a coffee at ko-fi.com" height="40" data-canonical-src="https://storage.ko-fi.com/cdn/kofi3.png?v=6" style="max-width: 100%; height: auto; max-height: 40px;">
  </a>
</p>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">What this is</h2><a id="user-content-what-this-is" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: What this is" href="#what-this-is"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">A structured workflow that turns Claude Code into a full-stack job application assistant. The core workflow (self-profiling, fit evaluation, and the drafter-reviewer application pipeline) is <strong>language- and country-agnostic</strong>. The job portal search skills are built for the Danish market (Jobindex, Jobnet, Akademikernes Jobbank, etc.), but the pattern is designed to be swapped for your local job boards.</p>
<div class="snippet-clipboard-content notranslate position-relative overflow-auto"><pre class="notranslate"><code>/setup          /scrape              /apply <url>
  |                |                     |
  v                v                     v
Fill in        Search job           Evaluate fit
your profile   portals              Score & recommend
  |                |                     |
  v                v                     v
Profile        Present matches      Draft CV + Cover Letter
files ready    with fit ratings     (LaTeX, tailored)
                   |                     |
                   v                     v
               Pick a match         Reviewer agent critiques
               -> /apply            -> Revise -> Final output
</code></pre><div class="zeroclipboard-container">

<p dir="auto">The framework encodes career guidance best practices, including structured evaluation criteria, forward-looking cover letter framing, and optional salary benchmarking.</p>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Prerequisites</h2><a id="user-content-prerequisites" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Prerequisites" href="#prerequisites"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<ul dir="auto">
<li><a href="https://claude.com/claude-code" rel="nofollow">Claude Code</a> (CLI)</li>
<li>Python 3.10+</li>
<li><a href="https://bun.sh" rel="nofollow">Bun</a> (for job search CLI tools)</li>
<li>LaTeX distribution with <code>lualatex</code> and <code>xelatex</code>: <a href="https://tug.org/texlive/" rel="nofollow">TeX Live</a>, <a href="https://tug.org/mactex/" rel="nofollow">MacTeX</a>, <a href="https://yihui.org/tinytex/" rel="nofollow">TinyTeX</a>, or <a href="https://miktex.org/" rel="nofollow">MiKTeX</a>. The CV compiles with <code>lualatex</code> (pdflatex often fails on modern MiKTeX installs with <code>fontawesome5</code> font-expansion errors); the cover letter compiles with <code>xelatex</code> because <code>cover.cls</code> requires <code>fontspec</code>. If using a minimal TeX install such as TinyTeX or BasicTeX, install the extra packages listed in <a href="/MadsLorentzen/ai-job-search/blob/master/SETUP.md#minimal-tex-install-tinytexbasictex">SETUP.md</a>.</li>
<li>Optional: <code>pdftotext</code> from <a href="https://poppler.freedesktop.org/" rel="nofollow">poppler</a> (macOS: <code>brew install poppler</code>, Debian/Ubuntu: <code>apt install poppler-utils</code>, Windows: <code>choco install poppler</code>) — used by <code>/apply</code>'s ATS parseability check on the compiled CV. If missing, the check degrades gracefully to a visual keyword review.</li>
</ul>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Quick start</h2><a id="user-content-quick-start" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Quick start" href="#quick-start"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>

<p dir="auto">PowerShell:</p>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-powershell notranslate position-relative overflow-auto" dir="auto"><pre><span class="pl-smi">$tools</span> <span class="pl-k">=</span> <span class="pl-k">@</span>(<span class="pl-s"><span class="pl-pds">"</span>jobbank-search<span class="pl-pds">"</span></span><span class="pl-k">,</span> <span class="pl-s"><span class="pl-pds">"</span>jobdanmark-search<span class="pl-pds">"</span></span><span class="pl-k">,</span> <span class="pl-s"><span class="pl-pds">"</span>jobindex-search<span class="pl-pds">"</span></span><span class="pl-k">,</span> <span class="pl-s"><span class="pl-pds">"</span>jobnet-search<span class="pl-pds">"</span></span><span class="pl-k">,</span> <span class="pl-s"><span class="pl-pds">"</span>linkedin-search<span class="pl-pds">"</span></span><span class="pl-k">,</span> <span class="pl-s"><span class="pl-pds">"</span>freehire-search<span class="pl-pds">"</span></span>)
<span class="pl-k">foreach</span> (<span class="pl-smi">$tool</span> <span class="pl-k">in</span> <span class="pl-smi">$tools</span>) {
  <span class="pl-c1">Set-Location</span> <span class="pl-s"><span class="pl-pds">"</span>.agents/skills/<span class="pl-smi">$tool</span>/cli<span class="pl-pds">"</span></span>
  bun install
  <span class="pl-c1">Set-Location</span> <span class="pl-s"><span class="pl-pds">"</span>..\..\..\..<span class="pl-pds">"</span></span>
}</pre><div class="zeroclipboard-container">

  Set-Location ".agents/skills/$tool/cli"
  bun install
  Set-Location "..\..\..\.."

<p dir="auto"><code>/setup</code> offers three paths: read your <code>documents/</code> folder if you have one populated (CV PDF, LinkedIn export, diplomas, reference letters, past applications), import a single CV pasted in chat, or walk through an interview. It auto-detects what you have and asks. Documents-folder mode is idempotent and safe to re-run as you add more material; see <code>documents/README.md</code> for the layout.</p>

<p dir="auto">This searches multiple job portals for positions matching your profile, deduplicates results, and presents them sorted by fit. Pick a match to run <code>/apply</code> on it directly — or, when a scrape returns more jobs than you want to eyeball, run <code>/rank</code> to batch-score them all against the fit framework and get a ranked shortlist first.</p>

<p dir="auto">This runs the full workflow: evaluate fit, draft CV + cover letter, review with a second agent, revise, and present the final output.</p>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Other commands</h2><a id="user-content-other-commands" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Other commands" href="#other-commands"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto"><code>/setup</code>, <code>/scrape</code>, and <code>/apply</code> form the core workflow. Seven more commands extend it once your profile is in place:</p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li><strong><code>/interview</code></strong> preps you for a scheduled interview on a tracked application. It builds a stage-specific prep pack from the application's archive (the exact posting, the CV and cover letter the interviewer actually read, feedback recorded from earlier rounds), researches the company and interviewers with a verify-before-use rule, maps likely questions to your STAR examples, and offers a mock interview following the roleplay protocol in <code>07-interview-prep.md</code>. Gaps get honest bridge answers, never invented experience.</li>
<li><strong><code>/outcome</code></strong> records what happened to an application - interview stages, offers, rejections, silence. It archives the submitted CV, cover letter, and posting text into <code>documents/applications/<company>_<role>/</code>, keeps <code>outcome.md</code> in the format <code>/setup</code> Path A parses, and updates the tracker. Once a few applications resolve, it points you back to <code>/setup</code> to calibrate the fit framework from what actually got interviews.</li>
<li><strong><code>/rank</code></strong> bridges <code>/scrape</code> and <code>/apply</code>: it batch-scores all newly scraped postings against the fit framework (parallel agents fetch each posting and score the five evaluation dimensions) and returns a ranked shortlist with honest per-job strengths and gaps. Deal-breakers veto, deadlines get urgency flags, dead postings get marked expired. Pick a number and it hands off to the full <code>/apply</code> workflow.</li>
<li><strong><code>/expand</code></strong> enriches your profile by scanning public sources you've already linked in it (GitHub repos, portfolio site, Kaggle, Google Scholar) and looking up syllabi for named courses and certifications. Discovered competencies are added to your profile with a source tag. Useful right after <code>/setup</code> to surface skills that documents alone don't make explicit.</li>
<li><strong><code>/upskill</code></strong> analyzes the gap between your profile and your tracked job postings (or a single posting via <code>/upskill <URL></code>). Produces a prioritized heatmap of skill gaps and a learning plan with web-searched study resources and time estimates. Useful for career planning between applications.</li>
<li><strong><code>/add-template</code></strong> registers your own LaTeX CV or cover letter template in place of the stock ones. It captures the template's instructions (compile engine, fonts, style rules, page limit), runs a mandatory test compile, and wires the template into <code>/apply</code>. See <a href="#latex-templates">LaTeX templates</a> below.</li>
<li><strong><code>/add-portal</code></strong> generates a job-portal search skill for a job board in your market. It investigates the portal (search URL pattern, result structure, access rules), scaffolds the CLI skill from the same structure as the shipped ones, and test-runs a live query before registering. See <a href="#job-search-tools">Job search tools</a> below.</li>
</ul>
<p dir="auto"><code>/reset</code> is also available, see <a href="#starting-over">Starting over</a> below.</p>

<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">How <code>/apply</code> works</h2><a id="user-content-how-apply-works" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: How /apply works" href="#how-apply-works"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">The <code>/apply</code> command runs a <strong>drafter-reviewer workflow</strong> with mandatory PDF compilation:</p>
<ol dir="auto">
<li><strong>Parse</strong> the job posting (URL or text)</li>
<li><strong>Evaluate fit</strong> against your profile (skills, experience, culture, location, career alignment)</li>
<li><strong>Draft</strong> a tailored CV and cover letter in LaTeX</li>
<li><strong>Spawn a reviewer agent</strong> that researches the company and critiques the drafts</li>
<li><strong>Revise</strong> based on the reviewer's feedback</li>
<li><strong>Compile and inspect</strong> both PDFs: lualatex for the CV, xelatex for the cover letter. Claude reads the rendered pages and iterates on the LaTeX until the CV is exactly 2 pages with no orphaned entry titles, and the cover letter is exactly 1 page with the signature visible and fonts consistent.</li>
<li><strong>ATS-check the CV</strong>: extract the PDF's text layer (<code>pdftotext</code>, optional dependency) and verify it the way an ATS parser sees it — contact details present as literal text, no garbled glyphs, sane reading order — then score the posting's keyword coverage against the extraction. Keywords the profile genuinely supports get added; genuine gaps stay visible, never stuffed.</li>
<li><strong>Present</strong> the final output with a verification checklist</li>
</ol>
<p dir="auto">All claims in the CV and cover letter are verified against your actual profile. The system never fabricates skills or experience.</p>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">What makes this workflow different</h3><a id="user-content-what-makes-this-workflow-different" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: What makes this workflow different" href="#what-makes-this-workflow-different"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<ul dir="auto">
<li><strong>PDF verification loop.</strong> Most LaTeX-resume templates produce "looks fine in the .tex" output that breaks in the PDF: job titles orphan to the next page, cover letters spill onto page 2, bullet fonts silently fall back to the body font. The <code>/apply</code> command compiles and visually inspects every PDF and applies targeted fixes (<code>\needspace</code>, <code>\enlargethispage</code>, font-matching wrappers for list items) until the layout is clean. This runs automatically on every application.</li>
<li><strong>ATS verification on the PDF text layer.</strong> An ATS reads the PDF's embedded text, not the rendered page — and LaTeX can silently produce PDFs whose text extracts as garbage (icon glyphs where the email should be, interleaved lines from multi-column layouts). <code>/apply</code> extracts the compiled CV's text layer with <code>pdftotext</code> and verifies contact details, reading order, and the posting's keyword coverage against what a parser actually sees. Honesty rule enforced: a keyword the profile doesn't support is acknowledged as a gap, never stuffed in.</li>
<li><strong>Relevance-weighted CV cutting.</strong> When a CV overflows 2 pages, the workflow does not cut mechanically from the "oldest" section. It scores each candidate line by (a) relevance to the target posting, (b) uniqueness in the document, and (c) whether the cover letter depends on it, and cuts the lowest-total-score line first. An older-role bullet that hits posting keywords survives ahead of a recent-role bullet that does not.</li>
<li><strong>Drafter-reviewer separation.</strong> The drafter writes; a second Claude agent, spawned with a fresh context, researches the company and critiques the drafts. The drafter then revises. This catches missed keywords, weak framing, and generic language that a single pass often leaves in.</li>
<li><strong>Token-efficient reviewer dispatch.</strong> The reviewer agent receives drafts inline rather than re-reading them, and the verification checklist runs once at the end of the workflow rather than being duplicated by both agents. Note: the new compile-and-inspect step in Step 5 spends some of those savings on PDF rendering and layout iteration — the workflow trades some end-to-end token cost for a real reduction in broken PDFs reaching the user.</li>
</ul>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Customization</h2><a id="user-content-customization" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Customization" href="#customization"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>

<p dir="auto">This re-runs the search configuration interview: which roles to target, which skills to search for, which locations, and which portals. It also suggests role types you may not have considered based on your profile.</p>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">LaTeX templates</h3><a id="user-content-latex-templates" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: LaTeX templates" href="#latex-templates"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">The CV uses <a href="https://ctan.org/pkg/moderncv" rel="nofollow">moderncv</a> (banking style). The cover letter uses a custom <code>cover.cls</code> with Lato/Raleway fonts.</p>
<p dir="auto">To use your own template instead, run:</p>
<div class="snippet-clipboard-content notranslate position-relative overflow-auto"><pre class="notranslate"><code>/add-template
</code></pre><div class="zeroclipboard-container">
    <clipboard-copy aria-label="Copy code to clipboard" class="ClipboardButton btn btn-invisible js-clipboard-copy m-2 p-0 d-flex flex-justify-center flex-items-center" data-copy-feedback="Copied!" data-tooltip-direction="w" value="/add-template" tabindex="0" role="button">
      <svg aria-hidden="true" data-component="Octicon" height="16" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" data-view-component="true" class="octicon octicon-copy js-clipboard-copy-icon">

<p dir="auto">Point it at your <code>.tex</code> file (plus any <code>.cls</code>/<code>.sty</code> files or bundled fonts). The command interviews you for the template's instructions — compile engine, fonts and where they live, style rules to preserve, hard page limit — stores everything under <code>templates/</code>, runs a mandatory test compile, and activates the template so <code>/apply</code> drafts from it. Templates are stored with <code>[PLACEHOLDER]</code> tokens instead of personal data, so they're safe to commit and share.</p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li><code>/add-template --list</code> shows registered templates</li>
<li><code>/add-template --use <name></code> switches between them</li>
<li><code>/add-template --use default</code> reverts to the stock moderncv / cover.cls templates</li>
</ul>
<p dir="auto">If you prefer doing it by hand, the manual route still works: update the guidance in <code>05-cv-templates.md</code> and <code>06-cover-letter-templates.md</code>.</p>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Job search tools</h3><a id="user-content-job-search-tools" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Job search tools" href="#job-search-tools"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">The four Danish CLI tools in <code>.agents/skills/</code> (Jobbank, Jobdanmark, Jobindex, Jobnet) demonstrate the pattern for building a job-portal integration for a specific market. If you're in a different country, run:</p>
<div class="snippet-clipboard-content notranslate position-relative overflow-auto"><pre class="notranslate"><code>/add-portal
</code></pre><div class="zeroclipboard-container">
    <clipboard-copy aria-label="Copy code to clipboard" class="ClipboardButton btn btn-invisible js-clipboard-copy m-2 p-0 d-flex flex-justify-center flex-items-center" data-copy-feedback="Copied!" data-tooltip-direction="w" value="/add-portal" tabindex="0" role="button">
      <svg aria-hidden="true" data-component="Octicon" height="16" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" data-view-component="true" class="octicon octicon-copy js-clipboard-copy-icon">

<p dir="auto">Give it your local job board's URL. The command investigates the portal (search-URL pattern, result-page structure, robots.txt/access rules), scaffolds a CLI skill with the same structure, commands, and output contract as the shipped ones, and test-runs a live query before registering anything. Auth-walled portals are declined, and portals with restrictive terms get a prominent personal-use-only warning in the generated skill. The generated skill is market-specific and lives in your fork; the generator itself is the universal part.</p>
<p dir="auto">Maintaining a fork adapted to your market or language? Add it to the <a href="https://github.com/MadsLorentzen/ai-job-search/discussions/78" data-hovercard-type="discussion" data-hovercard-url="/MadsLorentzen/ai-job-search/discussions/78/hovercard" aria-keyshortcuts="Alt+ArrowUp">Community forks & adaptations</a> thread so others can find it.</p>
<p dir="auto">For <strong>country-agnostic</strong> starting points outside Denmark, the repo ships two portal skills alongside the Danish demos:</p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li><strong><code>linkedin-search</code></strong> — built on LinkedIn's public, unauthenticated <code>jobs-guest</code> endpoints. Field-agnostic, <strong>zero runtime dependencies</strong> (runs with just <code>bun</code>), and takes the search location as an explicit flag, so it works for any market out of the box (<code>-l "Berlin, Germany"</code>, <code>-l "Mumbai, Maharashtra, India"</code>, <code>-l "Remote"</code>, …). Intended for <strong>personal use only</strong> — automated access is against LinkedIn's Terms of Service, so keep volume low. See <code>.agents/skills/linkedin-search/SKILL.md</code>.</li>
<li><strong><code>freehire-search</code></strong> — queries the <a href="https://freehire.dev" rel="nofollow">freehire.dev</a> aggregator's public REST API (JSON, no API key). Tech-focused (software, data, engineering, DevOps, remote), multi-market via facet flags (<code>--region</code>, <code>--country</code>, <code>--remote</code>), and <strong>zero runtime dependencies</strong>. Unlike the HTML-scraping Danish portals, results come back structured (skills, seniority, category). The backend is MIT-licensed and <a href="https://github.com/strelov1/freehire">self-hostable</a> — point <code>FREEHIRE_API_URL</code> at your own instance if you prefer. See <code>.agents/skills/freehire-search/SKILL.md</code>.</li>
</ul>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Salary benchmarking</h3><a id="user-content-salary-benchmarking" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Salary benchmarking" href="#salary-benchmarking"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">The salary tool works with any salary data you provide (union statistics, Glassdoor exports, personal research, etc.). See <code>tools/README_SALARY_TOOL.md</code> for the expected format and setup. If you don't have salary data, the salary step is simply skipped.</p>
